payload.summary:
REVENUE MANAGEMENT MASTER KNOWLEDGE BASE - May 2026

=== BEYOND PRICING PLATFORM ===

HEALTH SCORE (core diagnostic):
- Scale 1-100. <60 = urgent changes needed. 60-80 = room to improve. 80-100 = performing well.
- Measures booking pace vs. market competitors (profitability-based, not just occupancy)
- Use as weekly diagnostic tool per listing

BASE PRICE:
- Represents median annual rate (NOT a floor)
- Algorithm fluctuates above/below it based on demand
- Review every ~2 weeks; update after renovations, photo changes, review inflection points
- Test higher than feels comfortable - guest demand validates price

MINIMUM PRICE (floor):
- Absolute floor - gap fills will NOT go below this
- Set based on costs + target margin, NOT competitor pricing
- Should allow for profitable last-minute discounts without selling loss nights

KEY BEYOND FEATURES:
1. Dynamic Syncing - event-driven price updates (not scheduled). Triggers on: new bookings, cancellations, modifications. Eliminates stale pricing windows.
2. Search-Powered Pricing (Pricing Pro) - uses actual guest search data to surface demand BEFORE competitors react. Flags pricing gaps when searches occur without bookings.
3. Gap Fill - auto-reduces minimum stay to gap length when gap < minimum stay. Prevents orphan nights. ~75% of unaudited portfolios have unbookable gaps.
4. Last Minute Discounts - automated price reductions as check-in approaches. Set discount % and trigger window.
5. Reputation Factor - auto price adjustment after negative reviews. Severity-based + time-decaying. 1-star: ~24% discount. 2-star: ~20%. 3-star: ~14%. Recovers after 2 consecutive 5-star reviews. Protects peak dates.
6. Listing Benchmark Factor - prevents premature discounting on key dates early in advance
7. Seasonal Rollover - pricing rules that repeat annually
8. Neyoba AI - natural language assistant. Ask 'why did you set this rate?' Gets answers without leaving platform. Integrated across all pages as of March 2026.
9. Comp Set - auto-generated competitive sets, filters ghost listings. All users have access as of March 2026.
10. Pricing Recommendation Sharing - share pricing recs with owners for approval/rejection
11. Dynamic Overrides - manual intervention always available
12. Portfolio Grid View (Compact) - rapid pattern detection across many listings
13. Listing Groups (coming soon) - flexible multi-group property organization

BEYOND ALGO FACTORS: seasonality, day-of-week, local events, booking velocity, search demand, availability (via Dynamic Syncing). 340K listings in 7,500 cities. 5 years historical data. 365-day rolling forward horizon. 12+ month requires manual support.

=== STR REVENUE MANAGEMENT FRAMEWORK ===

CORE DEFINITION: 'Selling the right night, to the right guest, at the right price' - strategic discipline that DRIVES pricing tools, not just uses them.

THE PRICING TRIANGLE:
1. Demand factors: micro-seasonal patterns, events, booking window timing, YoY trends
2. Competition context: 5-10 true comps by size/quality/location/amenities, market position, supply dynamics
3. Cost floor: cleaning + maintenance + platform fees + opportunity costs + required margin

KEY METRICS (ranked):
1. RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room-Night) = Total Revenue ÷ Available Nights. PRIMARY metric. Can't be gamed.
2. Market Penetration Index (MPI) = (Your Occupancy / Market Occupancy) × 100. Alert threshold: <85% at 90/60/30 day checkpoints.
3. Pacing vs. last year (on-the-books for next 30/60/90 days)
4. Median booking lead time (use MEDIAN not average - outliers skew mean)
5. ADR - lagging indicator (booked nights only)
6. Occupancy - context, NOT optimization target. Sweet spot 65-80%.

PACING STRATEGY:
- High season: pace roughly even (inventory tightens naturally, no need to rush discounts)
- Shoulder season: pace slightly ahead
- Low season: pace 10-15% AHEAD to lock bookings before competitors discount
- If pacing 20% ahead YoY but market pacing 40% ahead = likely underpricing

PROPERTY-TYPE PACING BENCHMARKS (occupancy at day intervals):
- 1-2 BR: 40% at 90d / 70% at 60d / 90% at 30d
- 3-4 BR: 30% at 90d / 60% at 60d / 85% at 30d
- 5+ BR: 20% at 90d / 45% at 60d / 75% at 30d

MINIMUM STAY STRATEGY:
- Default baseline: 2 nights
- High season/peaks: more restrictive (protect longer-value stays)
- Low season: flexible (capture weekend getaways)
- Gap fill: ALWAYS on
- Last-minute (7-14 days out): drop to 1-2 nights automatically
- Beat market minimum by one night to capture filtered-out demand

BOOKING WINDOW MANAGEMENT:
- Myrtle Beach lead time: 87 days avg (vs 55 national avg) - guests plan further ahead here
- 1-2 BR condos: 20-40 day window
- 3-4 BR homes: 45-75 day window
- 5+ BR luxury: 60-120+ day window
- Identify when 80% of demand captures and price competitively DURING that window
- 80% of effort should be on dates 30-120 days out; automate last-minute

FIVE OPERATIONAL PILLARS:
1. Base Price Optimization
2. Minimum Stay Management  
3. Booking Window Strategy
4. Event & Seasonal Pricing
5. Performance Measurement (3 ways: YoY, vs. market, vs. comp set)

WEEKLY TIME ALLOCATION (58 properties ~10 hrs/week):
- 6 hrs: dates 30-120 days out (pricing, minimums, seasonal refinement)
- 2 hrs: MPI and pacing review
- 1 hr: owner communication
- 1 hr: market analysis

DAILY REVIEW (15-30 min): new bookings, market occupancy changes, competitor rates, calendar gaps
WEEKLY (1+ hr): full booking window, property performance, seasonal strategies, minimize stays
MONTHLY (1-2 hr): RevPAR trends, base price review, seasonal patterns

TOP MISTAKES TO AVOID:
1. Max price caps that limit peak upside (remove them)
2. Minimum stay gaps creating orphan nights (~75% of portfolios have these)
3. Single minimum price year-round (use seasonal minimums)
4. Managing too reactively (last 7-14 days only) vs. proactively (30-120 days out)
5. Treating Beyond as autopilot without driving strategy
6. Stale base prices (>4 weeks without review)
7. Portfolio-wide cookie-cutter rules (each property needs own demand profile)
8. Ignoring booking window - pricing too late after guests have already booked competitors
9. Judging property value subjectively vs. trusting market signals
10. Strict cancellation policies in soft markets (penalty outweighs gains 3-5x)

CANCELLATION POLICY (2026 landscape):
- Airbnb: auto-migrated hosts to 'Firm' + mandatory 24hr free cancel for <28 nights
- Vrbo: expanded guest protections, penalties for host cancels (10-100% by timing)
- Booking.com: guest picks flexibility at booking
- Strategy: stricter in high-demand areas; flexible in softer markets
- Track booking window distribution (not just cancellation rates) as leading indicator

REVIEWS & REPUTATION:
- 21+ reviews = 71% avg occupancy. 5 or fewer = 57%. 14-point gap BEFORE pricing.
- Superhost status = better search placement = more pricing flexibility
- Price adjustments after bad review should be targeted, time-limited, proportional
- Beyond's Reputation Factor handles this automatically

=== MYRTLE BEACH MARKET DATA ===

BEYOND PRICING DATA (most authoritative):
- Avg occupancy: 60% (national avg 58% - slightly above average)
- RevPAN: /night
- Avg booking lead time: 87 days (vs. 55 national avg - Myrtle Beach guests plan FURTHER ahead)
- #1 distribution channel: Direct bookings at 28% (unusual! National norm is Airbnb-dominant)

AIRROI 2026 DATA:
- Active listings: ~7,365
- Avg annual revenue per listing: ~,016-,343
- ADR: -
- Occupancy: 38-47% (annual average)
- RevPAR: 
- Avg stay: 4.6 nights
- Avg bookings/listing/year: ~19.9

SEASONALITY:
- Peak (Jun-Aug): 54-72% occ,  ADR, ,580/mo avg
- July: 78-82% occupancy, - ADR - strongest month
- Shoulder (spring/fall): 47% occ, ~,383/mo
- Low (Jan, Nov, Dec): 23-26.5% occ, -209 ADR, ~,712/mo
- January = weakest month

Tripadvisor ranked Myrtle Beach #1 U.S. destination for summer 2026.
Tourism down 10.8% in 2025, projected -3% in 2026 (market headwind).
Supply grew 120.7% year over year (AirROI data).

=== MYRTLE BEACH KEY EVENTS CALENDAR (pricing opportunity map) ===

MARCH:
- Can-Am Days (Mar 13-22): ~100,000 Canadian visitors/snowbirds. MAJOR demand driver.
- Run to the Sun Car Show (Mar 19-21): 3,000+ vehicles, one of Southeast's largest
- Myrtle Beach Marathon (Mar 7)
- Surfside Beach BBQ Festival (Mar 6-7)
- St. Patrick's Day NMB (Mar 14)

APRIL:
- Socastee Heritage Festival (Apr 11)
- Food Truck & Music Festival (Apr 10-12)
- Monday After the Masters at Barefoot Resort (Apr 11-13) - golf + Hootie concert
- Int'l Film Festival (Apr 21-25)
- Brookgreen Wine Festival (Apr 25)

MAY (CRITICAL PRICING WINDOW):
- Sun Fun Festival (May 1-3)
- Ocean Drive Music Festival (May 2) - NMB
- Myrtle Beach Classic PGA TOUR (May 7-10) - PREMIUM EVENT, national golf audience
- Bike Week Spring Rally (May) - MASSIVE. One of largest motorcycle rallies in US. Hundreds of thousands of visitors. Fill rates spike dramatically.
- Blue Crab Festival (May 16-17) - 44th annual, Grand Strand's Best Annual Event
- Memorial Day weekend - military appreciation + peak kickoff

JUNE:
- Carolina Country Music Festival / CCMF (Jun 4-7) - Blake Shelton, Luke Bryan, Post Malone. MAJOR demand spike. Fills NMB accommodation.
- Muzika Festival (entire June)

SUMMER (Jun-Aug): sustained peak. Family travel dominant.

OCTOBER (shoulder opportunity):
- Myrtle Beach Jeep Jam
- Little River ShrimpFest (Oct 10-11)
- Myrtle Beach Jazz Festival (Oct 16-18)
- Surfside Beach Family Festival (Oct 3)

NOVEMBER-DECEMBER:
- Nights of a Thousand Candles (Brookgreen Gardens)
- Multiple Christmas light shows
- NY Eve at Market Common (Dec 31)

=== REVENUE BENCHMARKS ===
- RevFactor clients: +18% RevPAR vs. comp set (165+ properties, 24 states)
- Active revenue management vs. passive: 15-30% improvement within 90 days
- Market outperformance (well-run): 18-22% above market during market-down periods
- Individual property gains with full optimization: 21-36% month-over-month RevPAR
-  ADR increase on 180 booked nights = ,800 additional annual revenue
- Uncaptured revenue at M operator: K-K annually

=== UNWIND-SPECIFIC OPERATING MODEL ===
- 58 active properties (use this not OwnerRez's 95 which includes inactive)
- Beyond Pricing is revenue tool (switched back from PriceLabs Mar 2026)
- Primary metric: Revenue per property, YoY
- Hard floor: ≥90% owner retention (~6 departures/year max at 58 properties)
- Bez proposes, humans approve - all pricing changes go through John before implementation
- Morning digest runs daily at 8:33am ET for John
- Direct bookings strong at 28% (above national norm) - protect and grow this channel
- Market headwinds: supply +120% YoY, tourism demand flat to -3%

Tags: revenue-management, beyond-pricing, str, vacation-rental, myrtle-beach, pacing, pricing, unwind
